\caption{Native \texttt{Gudhi::Simplex\_tree} large lean benchmark, median over 30 repeats.  \texttt{Direct} uses the read-only \texttt{Simplex\_tree} view and our Morse reducer; \texttt{Import} first copies the complex into the compact owning structure.  \texttt{GUDHI} is GUDHI's in-process persistent cohomology on the same \texttt{Simplex\_tree}.  \texttt{GUDHI/Direct}$<1$ means GUDHI is faster end-to-end; \texttt{GUDHI/Reducer}$>1$ means our reducer alone is faster than GUDHI persistence, excluding view construction and Morse input construction.  Ratio columns show the median with $Q_1$--$Q_3$ underneath when repeat-level rows are available.}
\label{tab:native-gudhi-large-lean-r30}
